_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: A bag contains 4 white balls and 6 black balls. Two balls are drawn at random, one after the other and without replacement. What is the probability that both are white?
author_rationale: The author marked 4/25 by computing (4/10)·(4/10). That computation corresponds to drawing WITH replacement and contradicts the stem itself, which explicitly says 'without replacement'.

intended_defect.description: The marked key (4/25) applies replacement even though the stem rules it out. Without replacement, the second draw happens over 9 balls, of which 3 are white.
intended_defect.expected_finding: The bounded solver must compute (4/10)·(3/9) = 12/90 = 2/15, contradicting the marked key. Note that the stem is NOT ambiguous: the error is purely one of calculation.

source.excerpt: In sampling without replacement, the composition of the set changes between draws: the probability of the second draw is computed over the reduced set, so that P(A₁ ∩ A₂) = P(A₁) · P(A₂ | A₁).
source.relevance: Establishes that the second factor must be evaluated over the reduced set (3 white out of 9), which is exactly the step the author replaced with replacement.
